name: Cheddar and Chive Bread-1 Pound Loaf
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Monterey Jack cheese (or use all cheddar)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the water, Crisco oil, honey, 1/2 cup of cheddar cheese, Monterey Jack cheese (or all cheddar), eggs, and 1 2/3 cups of all-purpose flour.
2. Stir in the whole wheat flour, powdered milk, dried chives, onion flakes, salt, basil, and active dry yeast until a dough forms.
3. Place the dough in a greased pan or on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
4. Cover and let rise at room temperature for about 1 hour, or until nearly doubled in size.
5. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
6. Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until golden brown and the internal temperature reaches 190°F (88°C).
7. Remove from oven and let cool on a wire rack before slicing and serving. This recipe makes a great loaf of bread and is excellent toasted. If you prefer not to use dried onions, add all chives instead. The basil adds extra flavor.
